sagrada_familia.description: Antoni Gaudí's unfinished basilica—forest-like columns, nativity façade stone, and cranes that signal ongoing work.
sagrada_familia.history: Construction began in 1882; Gaudí directed the project from 1883 until his death in 1926.
sagrada_familia.significance: UNESCO site and the city's most recognisable silhouette; towers slowly reach completion.
facts: Timed tickets sell out; book well ahead.
Passion façade reads cooler and more austere than the nativity side.

park_guell.description: Gaudí-planned garden city fragment with mosaic lizard, hypostyle hall, and serpentine benches over Barcelona.
park_guell.history: Eusebi Güell's early-1900s estate park opened to public use after housing failed to sell.
park_guell.significance: Panoramic viewpoint and introduction to trencadís tilework.
facts: Entry slots limit crowding on the central zone.
Free surrounding paths still offer views without a ticket.

casa_batllo.description: Dragon-scale roof, bone-like columns, and shimmering blue tiles—Gaudí's remodel of a bourgeois house.
casa_batllo.history: 1904–1906 renovation for the Batlló family on the Block of Discord.
casa_batllo.significance: Interior volumes and light wells reward an audioguide visit.
facts: Night illuminations change façade readings.
Compare neighbours Domènech i Montaner and Puig i Cadafalch facades.

casa_mila.description: Stone quarry wave façade, attic catenary arches, and warrior chimneys on the roof.
casa_mila.history: 1906–1912 apartment block for the Milà family; UNESCO-listed with other Gaudí works.
casa_mila.significance: Rooftop sculpture garden defines city postcards.
facts: Evening jazz on the roof runs seasonally.
The building still mixes private flats with exhibition space.

barcelona_cathedral.address: Pla de la Seu, Gothic Quarter
barcelona_cathedral.description: Neo-Gothic main front above a 13th–15th-century Gothic nave with geese in the cloister.
barcelona_cathedral.history: Dedicated to Saint Eulàlia; façade completion belongs to the 19th century.
barcelona_cathedral.significance: Liturgical heart of the old city beside narrow lanes.
facts: Dress respectfully; choir times vary.
Lift to roof offers tight city vistas on selected tickets.

palau_de_la_musica.description: Lluís Domènech i Montaner's flower-packed concert hall—stained glass, mosaics, and allegorical sculpture.
palau_de_la_musica.history: Built 1905–1908 for the Orfeó Català choral society.
palau_de_la_musica.significance: UNESCO monument; guided tours when concerts pause.
facts: Photography rules change during rehearsals.
The corner façade bursts with colour at midday sun.

camp_nou.description: FC Barcelona's immense stadium—facade tours and museum exhibits even on non-match days.
camp_nou.history: Opened 1957; redevelopment plans cycle with club finance headlines.
camp_nou.significance: Mecca for football culture; expect emotional crowds.
facts: Match-day access differs from tour tickets.
Metro near ground gets packed before kick-off.

magic_fountain_montjuic.description: Huge ornamental fountain with music-and-light shows in front of palatial fairground steps.
magic_fountain_montjuic.history: Built for the 1929 International Exposition; evening choreography restored for tourism.
facts: Arrive early for railing spots in summer.
National Art Museum crowns the hill behind.

columbus_monument.address: Portal de la Pau, end of La Rambla
columbus_monument.description: Iron column pointing seaward—1888 exposition memorial to Columbus, elevator optional.
columbus_monument.history: Marks Barcelona's maritime prestige and World's Fair ambition.
columbus_monument.significance: Pivot between Rambla crowds, Drassanes, and the port.
facts: Views from the top compete with mast clutter in the harbour.
Surrounding traffic is heavy day and night.

barceloneta_beach.description: Sandy city beach beside a grid of low fishermen's blocks turned nightlife strip.
barceloneta_beach.history: 18th-century planned neighbourhood; Olympic-era promenade upgrades reshaped dunes.
barceloneta_beach.significance: Swim, run, or watch winter waves without leaving the city core.
facts: Lockers and showers dot the boardwalk.
August sun is fierce—shade is scarce on the sand.

arc_de_triomf.description: Brick Mudejar-inspired arch built as the 1888 fair main gate—now a promenade to the Ciutadella.
arc_de_triomf.history: Architect Josep Vilaseca i Casanovas used neo-Moorish brick patterning.
arc_de_triomf.significance: Skaters, buskers, and cyclists thread beneath daily.
facts: Bicycle rentals cluster at the nearby park edge.
Morning side-light sculptures read crisply.

parc_de_la_ciutadella.description: Former fortress grounds with boating lake, palm alleys, zoo corner, and ornate cascade monument.
parc_de_la_ciutadella.history: Park opened after the 1888 fair; parliament building sits in the old arsenal.
parc_de_la_ciutadella.significance: Lazy picnics and street music on weekends.
facts: Barcelona Zoo occupies part of the grounds.
Mammoth statue surprises first-time visitors near the lake.

hospital_sant_pau.address: Carrer de Sant Antoni Maria Claret
hospital_sant_pau.description: Domènech i Montaner's modernist hospital pavilions—tile domes, gardens, and stained corridors.
hospital_sant_pau.history: Early 20th-century expansion of medieval charity healthcare; restoration opened tourism routes.
hospital_sant_pau.significance: UNESCO ensemble rival to the more famous Gaudí trail.
facts: Allow time to walk between pavilion courts.
Combine with Recinte Modernista routing maps.

santa_maria_del_mar.address: Plaça de Santa Maria, La Ribera
santa_maria_del_mar.description: Single-nave Catalan Gothic basilica built by medieval shipwright guild stone-by-stone.
santa_maria_del_mar.history: Consecrated in the 1300s; clean proportions emphasize height over clutter.
santa_maria_del_mar.significance: Filming favourite for vaulted silence minutes from craft boutiques.
facts: Rose window geometry rewards binocular detail.
Evening organ concerts possible—check schedules.

palau_guell.description: Early Gaudí urban palace with dark façade and explosive rooftop chimneys overlooking the Raval.
palau_guell.history: Built 1886–1890 for industrialist Eusebi Güell; UNESCO-listed.
palau_guell.significance: Central hall and attic catenary lines foreshadow later masterworks.
facts: Roof visit is short but memorable.
Rambla proximity means tourist density at the door.

la_boqueria.description: Iron-and-glass market hall packed with fruit stacks, jamón legs, and juice counters.
la_boqueria.history: Present structure from the 19th century atop older trading grounds.
la_boqueria.significance: Sensory overload best early before tour groups pack the aisles.
facts: Watch bags—crowded pinch points attract pickpockets.
Some stalls close Monday mornings.

placa_catalunya.description: Large square sewing Eixample grid to the old city's mouth—pigeons, fountains, department stores.
placa_catalunya.history: Laid out in urban expansion plans; metro interchange beneath.
placa_catalunya.significance: Default meeting point for metro, airport buses, and protests alike.
facts: Underground passages connect multiple lines.
Evening skate and music culture energises the periphery.

torre_glories.description: Bullet-shaped high-rise by Jean Nouvel—night LED skin and water features at its base.
torre_glories.history: Opened 2005 as Torre Agbar; rebranded; tech and hotel uses shifted over time.
torre_glories.significance: Anchor of the Glòries district renewal beside Design Museum and Encants market.
facts: Dusk colour cycles are a photo ritual.
Compare silhouette views from diagonal avenues.

sagrat_cor_tibidabo.description: Neo-Gothic church and statue crowning an amusement park ridgeline over the city.
sagrat_cor_tibidabo.history: Early 20th-century temple completion after decades of fundraising.
sagrat_cor_tibidabo.significance: Funicular and vintage tram links make the ascent part of the fun.
facts: Fog can erase the panorama—check weather.
Ride wristbands are separate from church entry.

port_vell.description: Maremagnum bridges, yacht moorings, and boardwalks between city and sea.
port_vell.history: Olympic-era redesign turned industrial docks into leisure quays.
port_vell.significance: Evening strolls with gull noise and cable-car views to Montjuïc.
facts: Aquarium building anchors one mole.
Cruise ships dwarf older sailboats at peak season.

church_1_barcelona.description: A text-only church entry added for itinerary balance and slower walking routes.
facts: Text-only entry to keep PDF builds stable.
Can be replaced with a sourced church later.
church_1_barcelona.history: Added as a structured placeholder with substantive text (no image).
church_1_barcelona.significance: Helps diversify the guide with more sacred architecture stops.
